text.title: Channel Research Agentic Pipeline
text.summary: Three-stage subagent chain, prompt pattern, tools and services, and agent gotchas specific to channel research.

list.item: Stage 1 — discovery agent: gathers customer attribution (interviews + analytics) + competitor channel evidence; uses faion-research-agent in competitors + personas modes
Stage 2 — scoring agent: applies channel-fit matrix and economic model; uses growth-marketer + ppc-manager knowledge for CPC/CTR benchmarks
Stage 3 — test-design agent: produces ranked test plan with budgets, timing milestones, and pre-registered kill criteria; checks with gtm-strategist knowledge for phase gating

rule.statement: When the agent has zero customer interviews, flag this explicitly in the output header so the reader knows the input quality — fall back to competitor inference with a clear "UNVERIFIED" label on every inferred channel.
rule.rationale: Channel research output reads plausible but un-actionable when built entirely from competitor inference without customer source data. The quality flag prevents the output from being acted on at the same confidence level as interview-grounded research.

example.note: Channel research prompt with citation and complexity requirements:
example.code: Role: distribution-channel research subagent. Inputs: ICP={...}, product={...}, competitors=[...], current_channels=[...], LTV_estimate=$X. Tasks: 1. Map top 5 customer-discovery sources (cite interviews/analytics/competitor evidence; refuse to invent numbers). 2. Score channels against fit matrix (audience, cost, time, scale, capability). Output table. 3. Model economics per channel using stated LTV and benchmark CPC ranges (cite source + date). 4. Recommend Phase-1 test plan: 1 primary + 1 backup, budget $500-2K, milestone-gated stop criteria. Constraints: no time estimates in days, only complexity (low/med/high). Flag any unverified numbers with [UNVERIFIED]. Return: markdown sections matching channel-report.md.

list.item: SparkToro API: audience hangout discovery (sites, podcasts, social handles) — feeds Step 1
SimilarWeb API: channel mix per competitor domain (search/social/direct/referral)
Meta Ad Library (public API): snapshot of active Meta/Instagram ad creatives
Google Ads Transparency (scrape): Playwright required, no formal API
ahrefs / semrush API: organic + paid keyword exposure, content gap analysis
PostHog / Plausible API: self-attribution data export + UTM analysis
serpapi / serper.dev: programmatic Google search for keyword volume signals
searxng (self-hosted): NERO runs SearXNG at ~/workspace/tools/searxng for agent loops

antipattern.description: LLMs hallucinating CPC, CPM, and conversion benchmarks without citing source and date.
antipattern.reason: Require the agent to cite source + date for every economic number. Reject answers that fail the citation requirement. CPC/CPM numbers shift 2x+ within 12-24 months.

antipattern.description: Agents over-recommending SEO and content marketing because training data is saturated with those examples.
antipattern.reason: Explicitly prompt for non-obvious channels (integrations, niche marketplaces, communities, podcasts) and require a justification for why standard channels were or were not selected.

antipattern.description: Last-click attribution baked into agent reasoning under-crediting dark social, podcast, and community channels.
antipattern.reason: Feed self-reported attribution data alongside analytics. Last-click alone produces wrong recommendations for top-of-funnel channels that have long conversion cycles.

antipattern.description: Agent committing to a Phase 1/Phase 2/Phase 3 calendar with specific dates or week counts.
antipattern.reason: Per workspace rules, no time estimates — phases are gated by milestones (e.g., "after 100 paying customers"), not weeks. Phase gates on outcomes prevent premature scaling.

text.reference: Weighted matrix scorer from YAML channel definitions.
Example input with three pre-scored channels.
